pattern: With at most a dozen elements, enumerating every subset and checking whether its product hits the target while its complement is non-empty and matches is fast enough.
hints: The whole array is split in two, so the total product must equal the target squared for any hope of success.
There are only a handful of elements; you can try every way of assigning each to one side.
Iterate over all bitmask subsets that are neither empty nor full; if the subset's product equals the target, the complement's product equals it too when the total is target squared.

edge_inputs.description: no subset reaches the target product
total product is target squared but no actual split works
presence of 1 lets it ride along either side
target reachable but complement empty is rejected

stress.description: small distinct multiset with target derived from a random valid split half the time
stress.note: distinct values per the constraint; half the cases plant a real split
